content: Just as the human language-learning definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of input and a deliberate cognitive反思 to refine understanding, existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by borrowing this dual framing: separating the passive 'reflection' of inconsistent solving perspectives (as documented in existing LLM research) from the active, deliberate cognitive reflexion that reinforces correct reasoning patterns. This creates a two-stage reflexion framework for LLMs that mirrors how human learners use reflexion in language learning to correct errors and reinforce mastered knowledge.
